observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts to properly reclassify the accounts in accordance with the prescribed Chart of Accounts to reflect accurate representation of City Government’s claims and financial records.
We recommended that the City Accountant continue his efforts in complying with the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements for fair presentation of Cash in Bank accounts of the City.

We recommended that the Management advise the City Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher for the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE amounting to P3,496,213.13 to the Trust Fund Book and submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher. We further recommended that the City Treasurer cause the transfer of the amount from the general fund to the trust fund bank account.

We recommended that the Management send written notices to the concerned contractors to inform them of their unclaimed retention fees recorded in the books of the City Government to establish the validity, existence, and correctness of the recorded payable accounts.

We further recommended that Management, upon determination that the concerned contractors are no longer interested in their respective claims, instruct the City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to revert back the identified long outstanding payables to the unappropriated surplus, pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 to establish the reliability of the account’s balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Review all accounts affecting the Government Equity under Trust Fund, prepare the necessary adjusting entries and transfer the identified items from Government Equity in the Trust Fund to the General Fund books; and
Make a complete disclosure of Prior Period Errors in the Notes to Financial Statements as required by Section 344, Volume I of Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to provide users of financial statements with adequate information in understanding the transactions of the LGU through the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant properly record all financial transactions using the appropriate account titles based on the actual description of transactions in conformity with the revised chart of accounts in order to permit Management to review its activities according to areas of responsibilities and allow for a clearer and fair presentation of transactions in the financial statements of the City.

Accordingly, we recommended that the City Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify all accounts charged to Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses (Other MOOE) account to the most appropriate accounts in accordance with the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Management strictly follow the rules in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002, and the following specific courses of action:
Require all concerned employees to immediately settle all unliquidated cash advances through the submission of necessary supporting documents. Otherwise, failure of the employee to liquidate his/her cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause by the Management to withhold of his/her salary or any money due him/her in accordance under paragraphs 9.3.2 and 9.3.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Require the issuance of a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advances to preclude accommodation of employees not complying with the regulatory requirements pertaining to liquidation.
Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances for expenses other than TEVs to employees unless they are designated as disbursing officers and properly bonded.
Reclassify/Record CAs for non-travel purposes to the proper accounts which are of the nature of Advances to Special Disbursing Officers or Advances for Operating Expenses in accordance with the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant make a representation with the identified contractors to request their submission of final billing to settle the City Government’s obligation and as well as recover the unrecouped advance payments made totaling P4,816,877.42. If there is pending valid claim not yet paid to the contractors, the City Accountant should hold such payment and apply the unrecouped amount of the advances to contractors.

We recommended that the management, through the City General Services Officer, prioritize the registration and transfer of land titles to solidify ownership and safeguard the City's interests, ensuring compliance with Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that Management continue to comply with the requirements of honorarium rates per procurement project. Moreover, we recommended that the City Accountant comply with the following:
Ensure that appropriate taxes are computed and deducted from payments of honoraria;
Transactions are recorded in the books using the proper account classification in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and
Submit the identified lacking documents and strictly comply with the documentary requirements on payments representing honoraria claims of personnel involved in procurement projects as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a.) 	Continue to uphold their mandate by promptly implementing priority development projects as outlined in approved local development plans, ensuring effective and efficient project execution to maximize developmental benefits for beneficiaries.
Encourage the City Planning and Development Officer to maintain close coordination with the City Engineer and Accountant for proper project monitoring and to ensure the accurate and timely preparation of the Status Report of Government Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) before submission to the Audit Team.
c.) 	Ensure that the City Engineer consistently implements Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) in a timely manner, in close coordination with the City Planning and Development Office.

We recommended that the City sustain its efforts to maximize the use of the SEF, ensuring proper allocation to priority programs, projects, and activities so as not to deprive its citizens of the benefits that can be derived therefrom and to maximize the attainment of the purposes for which the fund was created.

We recommended that Management hasten the utilization of the fund transfers from the NGAs and implementation of the intended projects to ensure effective delivery of services to the intended beneficiaries. Moreover, monitor the status of the other projects and its completion.

observations.recommendation: We strictly recommended that Management and its Local Finance Committee undertake an immediate review of the financial condition of the City Government and identify areas and propose measures to ease the cash deficit. Management should also endeavor to take immediate and necessary economy measures to address the incurrence of significant cash overdraft. Additionally, strictly stop the practice of using trust funds for payment of regular expenditures to avoid the incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the imbalance was brought about by the insufficiency of funds to complete the projects and activities, due to shortfall in the revenue collections. Since the projects have already been completed, Management resorted to apply the payment the following year. The City Government has already intensified its revenue collection efforts and was able to collect more than the last year’s collections. Management also resorted to reservation of ten (10) percent of each department’s budget to cover the possible shortfall in the collections. The City Government will also adhere to the other recommendations.
observations.reason: Restated thru AOM No.  2021-018 and included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant fully record in the books all obligations incurred for infrastructure projects completed and accepted on account in the period to which they relate in order to fairly present the financial position of the City Government in the financial statements as at December 31, 2019.
observations.managementAction: Accounting adjustments on the affected accounts in CY 2019 were effected in the ensuing year. However, Audit team continued monitoring transactions at year end of CY 2020 and noted the same observation, thus AOM No. 2020-026 dated March 5, 2021 was issued and included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We commended the City Government for its unending delivery of worthy services aimed at ensuring the safety and uplift the living condition of its constituents particularly during the COVID 19 crisis, as we recommended that the City Government continue to act within the letter and spirit of all the rules, regulations and directives issued by the national government and fully cooperate towards a unified, cohesive and orderly implementation of the national policy to address COVID-19. Appropriately, ensure that the funds released to the City Government are utilized strictly in accordance with applicable budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations, and pertinent provisions of the RA 9184 and it’s Revised IRR.

We recommended that Management (i) through the CDRRMO in coordination with the City Accountant, City Treasurer and City Budget Officer review the Report on Utilization of DRRMF. Prepare and submit the correct Report in order to reflect the true balances of the sources and utilization of the fund. Hereafter, submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (ii) submit an amended LDRRMFIP and Utilization Report for CYs 2017 and 2018 duly approved by the Local Chief Executive to reflect the balances of LDRRMF for the applicable years.

We recommended that Management (a) Direct the City Assessor and City Treasurer to exert extra effort in the proper preparation and reconciliation of their respective records to come up with a complete and accurate list showing the names of the taxpayers with the amounts due and collectible as basis of the City Accountant in setting up the correct Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables. Consequently, the City Accountant should record the RPT/SET Receivables using as basis the reports from the City Treasurer and City Assessor in accordance with Section 42, Volume 1 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs for a fair presentation of the balances in the financial statements. (2) Consider to immediately upgrade its real property tax system in order to allow the City Treasury Office and other stakeholders to utilize the full potential of the system and be able to generate the necessary reports and information to aid Management in decision-making.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Accountant provide for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Other Receivables Account in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 29. As to the other account receivables, after fully exhausting all means of collections, initiate legal actions to collect the receivables from former employees as appropriate.

We recommended that Management be guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, and accordingly:
the City Accountant (i) prepare subsidiary ledgers that would completely account for each item of PPE in the financial statements; (ii) provide a full and appropriate disclosure of the PPE account in the Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed in the GAM for LGUs and IPSAS and (iii) derecognize unserviceable properties from the books.
Require the Inventory Committee to reconcile the results of physical inventory count against the recorded amounts maintained by the Accounting Office; adjust the Investment Property and Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts in the books as necessary; prepare and submit the corresponding report together with the established subsidiary ledger for each class of PPE.
Facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in order to realize income from sale thereof and to be derecognized from the accounting records.
Complete and submit the Inventory of all Local Roads and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards, Report on Local Road Network of the City as shown in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008. The preparation of the Report of Local Road Network must be observed yearly. Also, keep and maintain the Local Road Network Property/Property Ledger Card for all roads and its components.

We recommended that the City Accountant evaluate all available data to validly determine the appropriate cost to be charged as Medical Equipment and appropriate expense account in conformity with the revised chart of accounts in order to permit Management to review its activities according to areas of responsibilities and allow for a clearer and fair presentation of transactions in the financial statements of the City. The City Accountant should also ensure that disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents are properly and completely accomplished, authorized and submitted.

We recommended that Management:
Require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to reconcile the balances of Due to NGAs per Subsidiary Ledger and Utilization Reports in order to establish the correct balance of Due to NGAs account in the financial statements of the City Government; Ensure that only expenses related to each project for which the funds were created are included in the Subsidiary Ledgers and Utilization Reports.
Immediately return to the source agencies all unused/unutilized balance relating to the fund transfers received from national government agencies especially those that have remained long outstanding.
All department heads concerned are enjoined to monitor the implementation of all projects funded by other national government agencies, particularly those programs and activities relating to their respective offices in order to achieve the ultimate goals and objectives of the fund which is to promote general welfare and provide basic services to the constituents.

We recommended that the City Accountant fully record in the books all obligations incurred for infrastructure projects completed and accepted on account in the period to which they relate in order to fairly present the financial position of the City Government as at December 31, 2020.

We strictly recommended that the City Government secure and submit the necessary documents supporting the purchase of lots in accordance with Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to legitimize its ownership over the lots and to avoid unnecessary problems in the future thus protecting the interest of the government.

We highly recommended that Management strictly stop the practice of using trust funds to cover the payment of other regular operating expenditures. The Local Finance Committee and the Office of the Treasury should exercise due diligence in managing the funds entrusted to the City Government.

We recommended that the BAC conduct a judicious review of all the representations and data contained in the eligibility requirements to ensure that contracts are awarded only to eligible bidders. All components in the computation of NFCC should be carefully reviewed especially the value of all outstanding or uncompleted portion/s of the projects under ongoing contracts, including awarded contracts yet to be started, coinciding with the contract to be bid because this factor can dramatically decrease the NFCC which can be enough reason for a contractor to be disqualified. Likewise, the BAC should strive to strictly observe the documentary and procedural requirements of the Procurement Law.

We recommended that Management work to align its guidelines on the grant of donation/financial assistance with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2019, highlighting the objectives of the program which is to guide officials and employees of the City Government in the effective and efficient delivery of services to individuals and families in crisis situation or difficult circumstances.
Through the City Social Welfare and Development Office, assess all the requests for financial assistance by way of Social Case Study Reports so that beneficiaries are properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents. Also, assist the applicants and require a complete submission of supporting documents as a basis of assessment to make sure that only the deserving ones are provided with the necessary financial assistance.
Through the City Accountant ensure that complete supporting documents are complied with for all financial assistance claims pursuant to Section 4 (6 and 7) of P.D. No. 1445 to preclude the suspension and/or disallowance thereof.

Accordingly, we recommended that the City Government revisit its guidelines in defining the limits of mobile telephone expenses that should be availed by national government officials who are stationed within the City with the end in view that all disbursements are reasonable and moderate and that government funds are spent solely for public purpose in order to preclude the disallowance thereof. We also recommended that Management include in the payroll a certification to the effect that such amounts were used in the performance of their respective duties and responsibilities.

Accordingly, we recommended that Management (a) assess its capability in granting additional allowance in the form of honoraria to national government officials and strictly comply with the requirements and limitations set in DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1. Make a separate payroll for the NGA officials entitled to receive honoraria with the proper nomenclature so as to avoid confusion in the nature of additional allowances granted as well as the recording of account titles in the books; and (b) immediately discontinue the payment of additional allowance to NGA officials not covered by the above-cited DBM guidelines, otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit. The Management should strictly adhere with the guidelines set forth by the Department of Budget and Management implementing certain provisions of RA 7160.

We recommended that:
Management stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials other than for travel and to officers and employees who are not designated as disbursing officers. Discontinue the practice of paying through cash advances for the procurement of various supplies and materials instead settle them through direct payments to the suppliers.
Concerned officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances immediately submit their liquidation reports otherwise Notices of Suspension will be issued.
Disbursing Officers stop using their cash advances to pay for the reimbursements of other officials and employees and should use their cash advances only for the purposes to which they have been made.
Management endeavor to strictly operationalize in all financial operations of the City Government the fundamental principal that all “Disbursements or disposition of government funds or property shall invariably bear the approval of the proper officials”. Therefore, all approving officials should affix their signatures on all Disbursement Vouchers, Obligation Requests and supporting documents with incomplete signatures in order to signify and give proof of their approval of the financial transactions.
The Disbursing Officers submit the lacking documents of the identified liquidation vouchers to the Office of the City Accountant and Office of the Auditor. Henceforth, Management ensure that the guidelines and supporting documents required for the different types of government transactions are complied with before allowing payment to preclude the suspension and/or disallowance thereof.

We recommended that the City Treasurer strictly observe the sequential/chronological use of checks in order to avoid unaccounted checks. Also, all checks issued should be recorded chronologically in the Check Register by fund. We also strictly recommended that the City Treasurer require the accountable officer in charge of the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms to properly and completely prepare the report in order to prevent the incurrence of loss and for easy monitoring on the receipt and utilization of all accountable forms under the custody of the City Treasurer.

We recommended that the City Treasurer deposit her collections daily and intact or not later than the next banking day with the authorized depository bank in order to safeguard the government funds from any possible loss or misapplication pursuant to Section 66 of the GAM and as prescribed under Section 69 of P.D. 1445.
We also recommended that Management review the amount of operating fund set up for the financial assistance program for easier and systematic disbursements and recording and to refrain from using the collections to pay for financial assistance claims. Further, discontinue the practice of accommodating checks of City’s employees and other individuals.

We recommended that Management through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) adopt the prescribed forms of the Philippine Bidding Documents, 5th Edition, August 2016 pursuant to Section 6.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, ensure that all bidding documents are complete in form and content to foster attainment of the principles of transparency, competitiveness and system of accountability on government procurement at all times.
We also strictly recommended that the BAC post every stage of the procurement process for all the procured projects and comply with the posting requirements prescribed under the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR and all relevant policies issued by the GPPB.

We recommended that Management through the City Accountant immediately submit the DVs and supporting documents totaling ₱366,108,945.08 to ensure prompt verification and preclude the suspension and/or disallowance thereof.

We recommended that the City Accountant continue preparing the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all its bank accounts and carefully reconcile bank balances against general ledger balances in order to reflect fair balances of the cash account in the financial statements of the City.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements for January to December 2020 for all funds were prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management request from the Commission on Audit for authority to write-off from the books, all accounts that qualify for de-recognition pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005. Meanwhile, the City Accountant should set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Other Receivables account in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 29. Otherwise, initiate legal actions to collect the receivables from former employees as appropriate.
observations.managementAction:
The request of the City Mayor for authority to write-off dormant accounts especially those accounts of deceased accountable officials was submitted. 
Other Receivables from other officers not already connected with the City Government were given notice and demand letter for settlement of their accounts.
observations.reason:
The request for authority to write-off dormant accounts was favorably granted in the amount of ₱95,545.22.
However, the City Government failed to set up provision for doubtful accounts, thus reiterated thru AOM No.  2021-022 and included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the above observations, we recommended to the   Management the following:
Require the Inventory Committee to reconcile the results of physical inventory taking against the recorded amounts maintained by the Accounting Office; adjust the Investment Property and Property, Plant and Equipment accounts in the books as necessary; prepare and submit the corresponding report together with the established subsidiary ledger for each class of PPE.
 
Complete and submit the Inventory of all Local Roads and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards, Report on Local Road Network of the City as shown in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008. The preparation of the Report of Local Road Network must be observed yearly. Also, keep and maintain the Local Road Network Property/Property Ledger Card for all roads and its components.

The City Accountant makes the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as illustrated in Annex B of COA Circular 2015-008 and as prescribed by IPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The inventory taking has been completed. The City GSO is currently performing reconciliation with accounting balances.
observations.reason: Reiterated thru AOM No.  2021-015 and included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
In this regard, we highly recommended that the City Accountant draw the accounting journal entry to correct the entry made to record the collections received from the Ilagan Water District in order to reflect a fair presentation of balances in the financial statements of the City Government:

We also recommended that henceforth, the City Accountant record the monthly collections received from the Ilagan Water District as debit to Cash in Treasury account and credit to Due from GOCC account and discontinue the practice of recognizing the collections as service income, rather, recognize the collections as a deduction from the Due from GOCC account. 

We further recommended that the City Government request to the management of the llagan Water District to issue a separate check drawn from the bank of the Water District and made payable to the City of Ilagan for their monthly payment of obligations to the City Government.
observations.managementAction: Accounting adjustments have been recorded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:

The City Accountant and City Treasurer update and reconcile the balances in their books and prepare the updated Utilization Reports for all funds transferred from national government agencies duly approved by the Local Chief Executive in order to establish the correct balance of Due to NGAs account in the financial statements of the City Government. 

Recompense the Trust Fund bank account for the amounts transferred and used for other purposes in the General Fund totaling ₱2,681,598.49.

Immediately return to the Source Agencies any unused/unutilized balance relating to the fund transfers.

The City Accountant should draw the adjusting journal entries to properly recognize all collections categorized as Miscellaneous Income and Permit Fees and Trust Liabilities in the books of accounts. 

The City Treasurer should deposit all receipts of fund transfers from national government agencies in the corresponding trust bank accounts and the City Accountant should record the same in the Trust Fund books. 

All department heads concerned are enjoined to monitor the implementation of all projects funded by other national government agencies, particularly those programs and activities relating to their offices in order to achieve the ultimate goals and objectives of the fund which is to promote general welfare and provide basic services to the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The unutilized funds transferred from other agencies were returned to the Bureau of Treasury, while others that are still implementable will be utilized according to the purpose it was intended.

Management committed to discontinue the use of trust funds for other purposes.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, the Due to NGAs account on GF and TF books still had an accumulated balance of ₱18,176,237.11 and ₱3,802,627.83, respectively, thus same observation was issued thru AOM No. 2021-023 dated March 5, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the City Accountant immediately submit the DVs and supporting documents totaling ₱47,992,062.07 to ensure prompt post-audit thereof. Moreover, the Management through the Office of the City Accountant and Office of the City Treasurer should exert time and effort to strictly submit the monthly financial accounts and reports of the City Government within the prescribed period in consonance with Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the identified DVs for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:

Approving authorities ensure that disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are properly and completely accomplished as prescribed.
Submit immediately the lacking necessary supporting documents listed above to avoid audit suspension or disallowance thereof.
Strictly observe the documentary and procedural requirements of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant (a) ensure proper tax due are withheld from payments of overtime in the prescribed manner and remit the same within the stipulated dates in compliance with the above-cited BIR regulation; (b) ascertain all disbursement vouchers for overtime payments are properly supported with the overtime authority, duly prepared and approved payrolls, and duly prepared and approved daily time records; (c) require the preparation of DTRs extracted from the Biometric System in order to establish the appropriate number of overtime hours rendered by employees; and  (d) draw a journal entry voucher to reclassify the entry made on the overtime payment of CSWD personnel.
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the audit recommendation and henceforth complied with the rules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of actions: (i) the City Mayor direct the concerned officers and employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances, otherwise withholding of salaries shall be contemplated to enforce collection as provided in the same Circular and/or Notice of Suspension shall be issued for all long outstanding unliquidated cash advances; (ii) the City Accountant shall issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advances, and (iii) Management strictly follow the rules in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to forestall the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters have been sent to accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to perform the following: Set up provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Other Receivables account in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 21. Maximize efforts on the possible recovery of the dormant receivables but if thereafter, the same will still prove futile, Management is encouraged to file the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts and accordingly comply with the conditions and documentary requirements for writing-off of dormant accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: No actions undertaken by the management. However, they committed to request for an authority to write-off.

observations.recommendation:
8. We recommended that the City  Accountant  continue  preparing  the
          monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all its  bank  accounts
          and carefully  reconcile  bank  balances  against  general  ledger
          balances in order to reflect fair balances of the cash account  in
          the financial statements of the City.

10. We strictly recommended that  Management  and  its  Local  Finance
          Committee undertake an immediate review of the financial condition
          of the City Government and identify areas and propose measures  to
          ease the cash deficit such as but not limited  to  the  following:
          (a) only programs, projects  and  activities  (PPAs)  specifically
          identified in the Annual Investment Plan should be prioritized for
          implementation; (b) all unused balances of appropriations  without
          reinforced  cash  should  not  be  obligated  at   year-end;   (c)
          expenditures should be limited to the amount of  available  actual
          revenues during the year; PPAs previously included in  the  Annual
          Budget should be less prioritized and controllable expenses should
          be minimized considering that actual  revenue  collections  during
          the year were deficient against  the  projected/targeted  amounts;
          and (d) accelerate local tax collection measures.

      11. Management should also endeavor to take  immediate  and  necessary
          economy measures to address the  incurrence  of  significant  cash
          overdraft. Additionally, strictly stop the practice of using trust
          funds for payment of regular expenditures to avoid the  incurrence
          of cash overdraft.

12. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation  that  Management
          request from the Commission on Audit for  authority  to  write-off
          from the books,  all  accounts  that  qualify  for  de-recognition
          pursuant  to  COA  Circular  No.  2016-005.  Meanwhile,  the  City
          Accountant  should  set  up  the  provision  for   Allowance   for
          Impairment in  order  to  present  fairly  the  Other  Receivables
          account in the  financial  statements  at  net  realizable  amount
          consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 29.

      13. As to  the  other  accounts  receivables,  enforce  collection  by
          sending regular statement of  accounts  to  the  concerned  former
          employees and ensure that the accounts  receivables  are  settled.
          Otherwise, initiate legal actions to collect the receivables  from
          former employees as appropriate.

7. We recommended to the City Accountant  the  following  courses  of
          action:

       8.  Draw  the  appropriate  journal  entry  voucher  to  records  the
          transactions affecting the Due from Other Funds and Due  to  Other
          Funds Accounts as follows:


             General Fund Books


             (DR) Due to Other Funds    2-03-01-010           323,432.12
             (CR) Prior Period Adjustment 3-01-01-020         323,432.12


             To take up replenishment from GF to TF the balances of
             Various projects in TF charged and paid from GF



             Trust Fund Books

             (DR) Due from Other Funds 1-2-04-050       2,335,333.67
             (CR)     Due     to      NGAs                      2-02-01-050
             2,335,333.67


             To take up receivable from GF for GF projects charged  against
             TF

       9. Likewise, eliminate  or  close  the  reciprocal  accounts  in  the
          Consolidated Financial Statements to  present  fairly  the  actual
          condition of the City Government of  Ilagan  as  at  December  31,
          2019. Disclose the details of the reciprocal accounts in the Notes
          to Financial Statements.

      10. Henceforth, exercise due care in  recording  and  classifying  the
          City’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances  of  all
          accounts are correct and accurate.

21. Require the  Inventory  Committee  to  reconcile  the  results  of
          physical inventory taking against the recorded amounts  maintained
          by the Accounting  Office;  adjust  the  Investment  Property  and
          Property, Plant and Equipment accounts in the books as  necessary;
          prepare and submit the  corresponding  report  together  with  the
          established subsidiary ledger for each class of PPE.

      22. Complete and submit the Inventory of all Local Roads and Road  Map
          and Local Road  Network  Property  Cards,  Report  on  Local  Road
          Network of the City as shown in Annex A of COA Circular No.  2015-
          008. The preparation of the Report of Local Road Network  must  be
          observed yearly. Also, keep and maintain the  Local  Road  Network
          Property/Property Ledger Card for all roads and its components.

      23. The City Accountant make the appropriate disclosures in the  Notes
          to Financial Statements as illustrated in Annex B of COA  Circular
          2015-008 and as prescribed by IPSAS.

11. We  recommended  that  the  City  Accountant  immediately  draw  a
          reclassifying journal entry to correct the entry  made  to  record
          the payments of progress billings for the Design and  Construction
          of City Hospital from Buildings –  Hospitals  and  Health  Centers
          account (1-07-04-030) to Construction in Progress – Buildings  and
          Other Structures account (1-07-10-030).

      12. We also recommended that all disbursements from the  general  fund
          be supported by the  duly  approved/certified  Obligation  Request
          Slip in order to prove the  existence  of  appropriation  for  the
          purpose.

8. We recommended that the City Accountant fully record in the  books
          all obligations incurred for infrastructure projects completed and
          accepted on account in the period to which they relate in order to
          fairly present the financial position of the  City  Government  in
          the financial statements as at December 31, 2019.

5. We recommended  that  the  City  Accountant  properly  record  all
          financial transactions using the appropriate account titles  based
          on the actual description of transactions in conformity  with  the
          revised chart of accounts in order to permit Management to  review
          its activities according to areas of  responsibilities  and  allow
          for a  clearer  and  fair  presentation  of  transactions  in  the
          financial statements of the City.

       6. Accordingly, we recommended that  the  City  Accountant  draw  the
          Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify all accounts charged to  Other
          Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (Other MOOE)  account  to
          the most appropriate accounts in accordance  with  the  prescribed
          Chart of Accounts.

12. In this regard, we highly recommended  that  the  City  Accountant
          draw the following accounting journal entry to correct  the  entry
          made to record the collections  received  from  the  Ilagan  Water
          District in order to reflect a fair presentation  of  balances  in
          the financial statements of the City Government:

          Debit  Other Service Income (4-02-01-990)      5,510,766.26
          Credit Due from GOCC (1-03-03-020)             5,510,766.26
                 To  correct  the  entry  made  to  record  the  collections
                 received from Ilagan Water District

      13. We also recommended that henceforth, the  City  Accountant  record
          the monthly collections received from the Ilagan Water District as
          debit to Cash in Treasury account and  credit  to  Due  from  GOCC
          account  and  discontinue  the   practice   of   recognizing   the
          collections as service income, rather, recognize  the  collections
          as a deduction from the Due from GOCC account.

      14. We further recommended that the City Government of Ilagan  request
          the management of the llagan Water District to  issue  a  separate
          check drawn from the bank of the Water District and  made  payable
          to the City of Ilagan for their monthly payment of obligations  to
          the City Government.

6. We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:

         a. The City Accountant and City Treasurer update and reconcile the
            balances in their books and  prepare  the  updated  Utilization
            Reports for all  funds  transferred  from  national  government
            agencies duly approved by the Local Chief Executive in order to
            establish the correct balance of Due to  NGAs  account  in  the
            financial statements of the City  Government.  The  established
            ending balance in the Utilization Report should reconcile  with
            the Subsidiary Ledger maintained by the Accounting Office.  The
            City Accountant should ensure that  only  expenses  related  to
            each project for which the funds were created are  included  in
            the Report.

         b.  Recompense the  Trust  Fund  bank  account  for  the  amounts
            transferred and used for other purposes  in  the  General  Fund
            totaling ₱2,681,598.49. Discontinue the practice of using trust
            funds for other purposes other than the  specific  purpose  for
            which it came into the possession of the City Government.

         c. Immediately return to the Source Agencies any unused/unutilized
            balance relating to the fund transfers.

         d. The City Accountant should draw the adjusting  journal  entries
            to  properly   recognize   all   collections   categorized   as
            Miscellaneous Income and Permit Fees and Trust  Liabilities  in
            the books of accounts.

         e.  The  City  Treasurer  should  deposit  all  receipts  of  fund
            transfers   from   national   government   agencies   in    the
            corresponding trust  bank  accounts  and  the  City  Accountant
            should record the same in the Trust  Fund  books.  Accordingly,
            all trust funds should be used only for the purposes for  which
            the funds were received.

         f.   All department heads concerned are enjoined  to  monitor  the
            implementation  of  all  projects  funded  by  other   national
            government agencies, particularly those programs and activities
            relating to their offices in  order  to  achieve  the  ultimate
            goals and objectives of the fund which is  to  promote  general
            welfare and provide basic services to the constituents.

7.  We  recommended  that  Management  through  the  City  Accountant
          immediately submit  the  DVs  and  supporting  documents  totaling
          ₱47,992,062.07 to ensure prompt post-audit thereof. Moreover,  the
          Management through the Office of the City Accountant and Office of
          the City Treasurer should exert time and effort to strictly submit
          the monthly financial accounts and reports of the City  Government
          within the prescribed period in consonance  with  Section  122  of
          P.D. 1445.

11. We recommended to the Management the following courses of action:

          a.  The  City  Accountant  compute,  deduct  and   withhold   the
             appropriate taxes before making any payments to suppliers  and
             the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed  period
             as required in Section 1 of  Republic  Act  1051  and  Revenue
             Memorandum Circular No. 5-2006.


          b. Approving authorities ensure that  disbursement  vouchers  and
             supporting documents are properly and completely  accomplished
             as prescribed under paragraphs 5 and 6 of P.D. 1445.


          c. Submit immediately the lacking necessary supporting  documents
             listed  above  to  avoid  audit  suspension  or   disallowance
             thereof.


          d. Strictly observe the documentary and  procedural  requirements
             of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA  Circular  No.  2012-001
             dated June 14, 2012.

8. We recommended that the City Accountant (a)  ensure  proper  tax
           due are withheld from payments of  overtime  in  the  prescribed
           manner and  remit  the  same  within  the  stipulated  dates  in
           compliance with the above-cited BIR  regulation;  (b)  ascertain
           all disbursement vouchers for  overtime  payments  are  properly
           supported  with  the  overtime  authority,  duly  prepared   and
           approved payrolls, and duly prepared  and  approved  daily  time
           records; (c) require the preparation of DTRs extracted from  the
           Biometric System in order to establish the appropriate number of
           overtime hours rendered by employees; and  (d)  draw  a  journal
           entry voucher to reclassify  the  entry  made  on  the  overtime
           payment of CSWD personnel from account Repairs and Maintenance –
           Transportation Equipment (5-02-13-060) to account  Overtime  and
           Night Pay (5-01-02-130).

9. We recommended the following courses  of  actions:  (i)  the  City
          Mayor direct the concerned officers and employees to settle  their
          unliquidated cash  advances,  otherwise  withholding  of  salaries
          shall be contemplated to enforce collection  as  provided  in  the
          same Circular and/or Notice of Suspension shall be issued for  all
          long  outstanding  unliquidated  cash  advances;  (ii)  the   City
          Accountant shall issue a  certification  that  the  previous  cash
          advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books  before
          granting additional cash advances, and (iii)  Management  strictly
          follow the rules in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
          advance as prescribed in COA  Circular  No.  97-002  in  order  to
          forestall the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the City Accountant continue to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all its bank accounts and carefully reconcile bank balances against general ledger balances in order to present a fair presentation of the cash accounts in the financial statements of the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Require the Inventory Committee to fast track the completion of the physical inventory of PPE to determine the actual existence, condition and whereabouts of all properties owned by the Municipality; reconcile the accounting and inventory records; adjust the PPE accounts in the books as necessary; prepare and submit the corresponding report together with the established subsidiary ledger for each class of PPE. Complete the reconciliation of the RPCLRN and the updated Registry of Public Infrastructures through a detailed review of prior year’s transactions in order to identify the entire road networks of the City. Complete Local Road Inventory records with Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards; and (b) submit the Report on Local Road Network of the LGU prepared by the Inventory Committee as shown in Annex A of the circular. Make proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as regards all road networks in pursuance of COA Circular 2015-008.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Treasurer develop mechanisms to improve its deposit schedules in order to safeguard the funds of the City in conformity with the provisions of Section 69 (1) of PD 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The City has set up a petty cash fund to cater emergency transactions such as financial assistance granted to individuals in crisis situations. Thus, management has minimized delays in clearing deposits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly follow the rules in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to forestall the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. Accordingly, the City Government should: Require all concerned employees to immediately settle all unliquidated cash advances through the submission of necessary supporting documents. Otherwise, failure of the employee to liquidate his/her cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his/her salary and institution of sanctions provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Discontinue the granting of cash advance for mid-year bonus and other employee benefits. Discontinue the granting of cash advances to elective officials for purposes other than for travelling expenses. Moreover, stop the practice of allowing cash advances without the specified legal purpose.
observations.managementAction: The management has continued to pursue the settlements of the concerned individuals. They have issued demand letters and called their attention.

Prepare and submit monthly, a Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor, supported with signed and approved trip tickets to include data on the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the LGU during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle; and Require the driver of each of the motor vehicle of the LGU to prepare at the end of the month, a Monthly Report of Official Travels, summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month. observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the Office of the City Accountant continue to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all its bank accounts and carefully reconcile bank balances against general ledger balances in order to present a fair presentation of the cash accounts in the financial statements of the City.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to perform the following:
Set up provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Other Receivables account in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 21.
Maximize efforts on the possible recovery of the dormant receivables but if thereafter, the same will still prove futile, Management is encouraged to file the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts and accordingly comply with the conditions and documentary requirements for writing-off of dormant accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Based on the above observations, we recommended that Management:
Require the Inventory Committee to fast track the completion of the physical inventory of PPE to determine the actual existence, condition and whereabouts of all properties owned by the Municipality; reconcile the accounting and inventory records; adjust the PPE accounts in the books as necessary; prepare and submit the corresponding report together with the established subsidiary ledger for each class of PPE.
Complete the reconciliation of the RPCLRN and the updated Registry of Public Infrastructures through a detailed review of prior year’s transactions in order to identify the entire road networks of the City.
Complete Local Road Inventory records with Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards; and (b) submit the Report on Local Road Network of the LGU prepared by the Inventory Committee as shown in Annex A of the circular.
Make proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as regards all road networks in pursuance of COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that Management through the Office of the City Treasurer develop mechanisms to improve its deposit schedules in order to safeguard the funds of the City in conformity with the provisions of Section 69 (1) of PD 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the rules in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to forestall the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. Accordingly, the City Government should:
Require all concerned employees to immediately settle all unliquidated cash advances through the submission of necessary supporting documents. Otherwise, failure of the employee to liquidate his/her cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his/her salary and institution of sanctions provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Discontinue the granting of cash advance for mid-year bonus and other employee benefits.
Discontinue the granting of cash advances to elective officials for purposes other than for travelling expenses. Moreover, stop the practice of allowing cash advances without the specified legal purpose.

In view of the above observation, we recommended that Management require the contractors to submit any of the Irrevocable Standby Letter of Credit or equivalent value from a commercial bank /Surety Bond Callable upon Demand/Bank Guarantee as a support to mobilization fees paid and strictly observe the documentary and procedural requirements of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

In this regard, we recommended that Management deduct the retention money from progress billings paid for all projects implemented through competitive bidding prescribed under Items 6.1 and 6.2, Annex E of the 2016 revised IRR of RA 9184.
Management immediately submit the lacking necessary supporting documents as enumerated above in order to complete the documentation of the disbursement process.
We recommended that Management especially the approving authorities ensure that disbursement vouchers are properly and completely accomplished as prescribed under Section 4(5) of P.D. 1445.

In view of the above observations, we recommended that:
Management through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit the identified lacking documentation requirements to support the procurement activities conducted and partial payments made and strictly observe the documentary and procedural requirements of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Management require the contractors concerned to immediately return the excess amount of contract cost or deduct from its progress billings in order to avoid the issuance of notice of disallowance thereof.
Management submit the correct Statements of Work Accomplished together with the certificate of payment by the City Engineer that coincide and correlate with the disbursements paid to the contractors pursuant to paragraph 5 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor, City Vice Mayor,  and City Administrator instruct concerned officials and employees of the agency to immediately stop the practice of disintegrated procurement of office supplies and to require all offices under their respective supervision to carefully plan their office supplies requirement for a year or a quarter, submit in advance their procurement requests to the CGSO for purposes of procuring the same from PS-DBM, monitor the three-month office supply requirement and immediately recommend for the replenishment when stocks fall below the minimum levels so that occurrence of emergency procurement is minimized, if not avoided.

We also recommended that the City Mayor, City Vice Mayor, and the City Administrator approve procurement/payment of office supplies from local suppliers only after it was established that CGSO exhausted all means in procuring from PS-DBM but to no avail and provided that a certification of non-availability of stock is secured from PS-DBM; and that the CGSO course all procurement of office supplies to PS-DBM and request for certification of non-availability of stock therefrom as the case may be.

We recommended that (a) Management continue to comply with the requirements of honorarium rates per procurement project, thus should only be paid for successfully completed procurement through public bidding; (b) payrolls representing honoraria claims of personnel with involvement in procurement projects are supported with the necessary documents as stated in the COA Circular No. 2012-01. The Office of the City Accountant should ensure the completeness of the DV and other supporting documents to prove the validity and legality of the honoraria claims; (c) appropriate taxes are computed and deducted from payments of honoraria in accordance with the Revenue Regulation 08-2018 and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period as required in Section 2.79 (A) of Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce direct payment to   be made in check to suppliers and creditors for all purchases of goods and services as prescribed under Section 93 of P.D. 1445 and comply with BIR regulations on the withholding of appropriate tax from income payments to suppliers. Management should prohibit the practice of procuring goods and services through reimbursement unless in emergency cases or the items or services are petty in nature.

We recommended that Management through the Office of the City Accountant and Office of the City Treasurer submit the identified DVs and supporting documents in order to avoid audit suspensions thereof. We also recommend that Management submit the monthly financial accounts and reports within the reglementary period as required under Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Management ensure all procurements of the City Government are supported with complete documentation to prove the legality and propriety of the transactions. Accordingly, immediate submission of the lacking documents is enjoined for proper evaluation in order to comply with the documentation requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 on procurement of general support services.

We recommended the Management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977, among others:
Use the prescribed driver’s trip tickets and require the drivers/heavy equipment operators to present a duly accomplished and approved Driver’s Trip Tickets and an official travel or use of heavy equipment duly signed by passenger/user.
Prepare and submit monthly, a Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor, supported with signed and approved trip tickets to include data on the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the LGU during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle; and
Require the driver of each of the motor vehicle of the LGU to prepare at the end of the month, a Monthly Report of Official Travels, summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month.  The Report shall be certified by the driver and approved by the LCE or her authorized representative.

We recommended that following courses of action:
Instruct the Disbursing Officer to require the individual payees to present any valid or government issued identification card to ensure payment to the right person and demand authority/power of attorney if made thru a representative;
Management require JO employees to comply with the documentary requirements such as the submission of approved DTRs and accomplishment reports duly verified by the immediate supervisor for proper evaluation and documentation; and
Direct the Accounting Office to check the completeness and correctness of supporting documents before processing of claims for payment.
Accordingly, we recommended that Management strictly enforce the full settlement of the disallowed transactions within the required period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Sangguniang Panglungsod should adhere strictly
      to  the  aforecited  regulations.  The  members  of  the   Sangguniang
      Panglungsod should stop directly  granting  financial  assistance  but
      instead  course  through  or  recommend  the  requests  for  financial
      assistance from their constituents to the City Mayor  for  appropriate
      action.


   We also recommended that the City  Mayor  to  designate  a  disbursing
      officer to take charge  for  the  purpose  of  granting  of  financial
      assistance duly approved by the City  Mayor  or  her  duly  authorized
      representative. A petty  cash  fund  shall  be  established  for  this
      purpose using the imprest fund system.


   Likewise, management should prohibit the practice of  procuring  goods
      and services  thru  reimbursement  unless  item/service  is  petty  in
      nature. Strictly adhere with  the  guidelines  and  principles  on  an
      imprest fund wherein regular, recurring and non-petty expenses  should
      all be paid in  check  in  the  name  of  the  supplier  for  internal
      purposes.

We recommended that the City Local  Chief  Executive  to  require  all
      employees concerned to submit the necessary  supporting  documents  to
      prove the regularity, validity and legality of transactions and direct
      the City Accountant to assign one of the accounting personnel to check
      the completeness and propriety of the  supporting  documents  and  the
      City Treasurer make sure that  the  only  transactions  with  complete
      supporting documents be processed and paid to avoid  audit  suspension
      or disallowance as the case maybe.

We recommended that the City Mayor through the City Treasury Office to
      establish Petty Cash Fund (PCF) and to take charge for  each  type  of
      expenses using the  imprest  fund  system  to  avoid  or  curtail  the
      incurrence transactions on reimbursement. Management  should  prohibit
      the practice of procuring goods  and  services  through  reimbursement
      unless item service is petty  in  nature.  Strictly  adhere  with  the
      guidelines and principles on  imprest  fund  system  wherein  regular,
      recurring and non-petty expenses should be paid in check in  the  name
      of the supplier in compliance with the aforecited regulation.

We recommended that the City  Mayor  require  the  City  Treasurer  to
      deposit directly all her collections accruing   to  Special  Education
      Fund (SEF)  in the depository bank account maintained for SEF to avoid
      possible misappropriation and to  strictly  adhere  to  the  aforcited
      provisions on collections pursuant to Section 86, Volume I of NGAS for
      LGUs.

We recommended that the City  Chief  Executive  to  require  the  City
      Treasurer to remit the unexpended balances of  PDAF  to  the  National
      Treasury  since  the  fund  was  already  covered  by  the   permanent
      injunction  of  the  Supreme  Court  pursuant   to   the   afore-cited
      regulation.

We recommended that  the  Chief  Executive  advise  through  the  City
      Treasurer, that  the  Revenue  and  Market  Collectors  to  apply  for
      fidelity bond with the Bureau  of  Treasury  and  in  accordance  with
      Section 305 (f) of R.A. 7160 and Item  7.1  COA  Circular  No.  97-002
      dated February 10, 1997.


   We also recommended that, if these Accountable Officer refuse  to  be
      bonded since our recommendation seems to be ignored in every year cash
      examination conducted, the unbounded accountable  officers  be  relief
      from being a collector and designate other  accountable  officers  for
      their replacement.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive adopt strict  measures  to
      regulate  the  granting  of  cash  advances  in  accordance  with  the
      provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997  and adhere
      to Item 6.0 COA Circular 2012-004. Any unused or unspent cash advances
      should be returned or refunded.


   We further recommend that the City  Accountant  strictly  monitor  the
      settlement of unliquidated  cash  advances  and  discontinue  granting
      additional cash advances unless all  outstanding  cash  advances  have
      been  liquidated.   Subsequently,   officials   and   employees   with
      outstanding cash advances who get separated from  the  service  should
      not be cleared from money/property accountability unless  liquidations
      are made. We also recommend  to  stop  granting  of  cash  advance  to
      officers and employees which position/designation does not  define  as
      accountable officers as stated in the aforementioned circular.

We recommended  that  the  City  Accountant  to  file  a  request  for
      authority to write-off this receivable account with the Commission  on
      Audit.

We recommended that  the  City  Accountant  record  all  purchases  of
      supplies and materials to the appropriate  asset  accounts  using  the
      perpetual inventory method regardless of whether  the  items  will  be
      consumed within the accounting period or not.


   We also recommended that management require the property custodians of
      all requisitioning departments/offices to conduct  physical  inventory
      every semester and  to  submit  a  report  thereon  to  the  CGSO  for
      consolidation, so that the costs of supplies and materials actually on
      hand and issued/ consumed may be accurately determined and taken up in
      the books. The CGSO prepares the  Report  of  Supplies  and  Materials
      Issued (RSMI) to be submitted to the CAO as  basis  for  the  periodic
      recognition of inventory expenses.

We recommended to management to require the Accounting Office and the
      General Services Office to reconcile their respective records and  to
      come up  with  the correct balances of  the  Property,  Plant  and
      Equipment accounts.

We recommended that management require  the  BAC  for  submission  of
      contract  agreements  together  with  complete  supporting  documents
      within five (5) days after its perfection to facilitate our audit and
      to avoid  audit  suspensions.  We  request  all  copies  of  contract
      agreements  be  submitted  to  the  Office  of  the   Auditor.   “Any
      unjustified failure of  the  officials  and  employees  concerned  to
      comply with the requirements herein imposed shall be subject  to  the
      administrative disciplinary action provided in  (a)  Section  127  of
      Presidential Decree No. 1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-B,  Book  V  of
      the Revised Administrative Code  of  1987;  and  (c)  Section  11  of
      Republic Act No. 6713.”

We recommended that management  require the General  Service  Office
       for submission of  purchase  orders  together  with  the  supporting
       documents within five (5) days after its  perfection  to  facilitate
       our audit and to avoid  suspension  in  audit  suspensions  and  the
       penalty prescribed by law under  Item  4.1  of  the  Circular  which
       states “Any unjustified  failure  of  the  officials  and  employees
       concerned to comply with the requirements herein  imposed  shall  be
       subject to the administrative disciplinary action  provided  in  (a)
       Section 127 of Presidential Decree No.1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-
       B, Book V of the  Revised  Administrative  Code  of  1987;  and  (c)
       Section 11 of Republic Act No. 6713.”

We recommended that management adhere the provision of  Section  325
       (a) of Republic Act 7160 and provision of DILG  Memorandum  Circular
       No. 2004-13 dated October 1, 2004 and avoid  charging  of  wages  of
       casuals, job orders and contractual against MOOE;


    We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human
       Resource Management Office to evaluate the actual accomplishments of
       the casuals, job orders and contractuals to avoid duplicate of  work
       and submit the post evaluation report to the audit team.  The report
       should include if necessary, justification that the service rendered
       by the additional work force are indispensable for the  day  to  day
       operation of the LGU to warrant the continuity  of  their  services.
       Require also that all personnel whether permanent, casual, job order
       or contractual to log in  and  out  using  the  biometric  equipment
       provided to ensure their attendance while those  in  field  work  or
       leaving their respective area of assignment should be  covered  with
       travel order or locator slip as the case  may  be,  duly  signed  by
       their immediate supervisor and approved by the  City  Mayor  or  her
       representative or by the City Vice Mayor,  City  Councilors  as  the
       case may be.

We recommended that  management  require  the  CDRRMC  to  regularly
       prepare a Report on the Utilization of the  LDRRMF  and  submit  the
       same to the COA Auditor. Likewise, require the CDRRMC  to  have  the
       report public through publication and posting in conspicuous  places
       and its website. Also, require the City Accountant  to  comply  with
       the  reportorial  requirements  on  the  Accounting  and   Reporting
       Guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
       (LDRRMF) of the LGU, National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
       Fund (NDRRMF) given to LGUs and Receipts from Other Sources.

We recommended that the City Accountant should  maintain  a  Special
       Account in the General Fund on  for  each  economic  enterprises  to
       conform with the rules and regulations with Section 105, 106 and 107
       of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the City Chief Executive require the  Accounting
       Office, Treasury Office and  other  concerned  offices  to  strictly
       adhere  with  the  requirements  on  the  submission  of   financial
       statements and other financial reports as well as paid  disbursement
       vouchers and payrolls pursuant to Section 7.2 (a)  and  (b)  of  COA
       Circular 2009-006.


    We also  recommended  that  management  require  the  all  concerned
       offices to follow the proper steps in  processing  vouchers/payrolls
       as well as recording and payment  or  release  of  claims to  avoid
       missing and unsubmitted vouchers/payrolls.

The City Government adopt strict measures to       regulate the granting of cash advances in       accordance with the provisions of COA       Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and       adhere to Item 6.0 COA Circular 2012-004. Any       unused or unspent cash advances should be       returned or refunded.

The City Accountant      strictly monitor the settlement of unliquidated cash      advances and discontinue granting additional cash      advances unless all outstanding cash advances have      been liquidated.
